channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mani Shankar Aiyar Criticises Congress Support for TVK in Tamil Nadu
item.description: Veteran Congress leader Mani Shankar Aiyar has strongly criticised his party's decision to support actor-politician Vijay and his Tamilaga Vetri Kazhagam (TVK) in Tamil Nadu. Calling the move “dreadfu...

item.title: Influencer Murugi Munyi Appeals to Motorists to Prioritise Ambulances After Uncle’s Passing
item.description: Social media influencer Murugi Munyi has made an emotional appeal to Kenyan motorists to always give way to ambulances, following the death of her uncle, the late Dr Job Obwaka, who served as the Nair...

item.title: EFF's Malema calls on Ramaphosa to step down amid Constitutional Court ruling on Phala Phala impeachment process
item.description: Economic Freedom Fighters leader Julius Malema has urged President Cyril Ramaphosa to resign immediately so he can focus on defending himself in the impeachment proceedings following a significant Con...

item.title: 101 Aspirants Submit Certificates of Candidacy for BARMM District Representative Positions
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) reported that a total of 101 individuals have filed their certificates of candidacy (COCs) for district representative positions in the upcoming Bangsamoro Autono...

item.title: Lagos State Security Trust Fund: Model for Effective Funding and Public Safety
item.description: For Nigeria, securing lives and property na one serious matter, and e dey top list of government responsibilities. Na why Lagos State Government set up Security Trust Fund back in 2007, to tackle shor...

item.title: Widespread Canvas Outage Disrupts Thousands of Schools After Ransomware Attack
item.description: On May 8, 2026, thousands of schools across the United States experienced major disruptions after Instructure, the company behind the widely used digital learning platform Canvas, was targeted by a ra...

item.title: South Korean temple ordains humanoid robot to engage younger Buddhists
item.description: Jogyesa Temple in Seoul has recently conducted an unprecedented Buddhist initiation for a humanoid robot named Gabi, reflecting the Jogye order's efforts to modernise and engage younger generations am...

item.title: Regina School Criticized After Students Attend Mature LandBack-Themed Theatre Performance
item.description: A Regina school recently faced criticism after sending students to a performance of 'Little Red Warrior And His Lawyer,' a satirical, bawdy LandBack-themed play. The school had relied on the theatre's...

item.title: Ex-Anambra governor’s media aide declares for Njikoka 1 Assembly seat
item.description: Emeka Ozumba, wey be former media aide to ex-Governor Willie Obiano of Anambra State, don officially declare say him wan contest for Njikoka 1 State Constituency seat for Anambra State House of Assemb...

item.title: Scottish Parliament 2026: Early Results Show SNP Leading and Voter Turnout Updates
item.description: The 2026 Scottish Parliament election is currently underway, with voters electing 73 constituency MSPs and 56 regional MSPs across eight lists. Unlike previous elections, the vote count is taking plac...

item.title: Appeals body clarifies parking fine rules amid operators withdrawing cases
item.description: Recent developments in the regulation of parking fines have been clarified by the relevant appeals body, shedding light on the rules that govern disputed parking tickets. Over time, private parking op...

item.title: US Pentagon Publishes Declassified UFO Files Including Historical Sightings
item.description: On Friday, the US Pentagon released a collection of previously classified files documenting sightings of unidentified flying objects, known officially as Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAPs), some ...

item.title: Former Botswana President Festus Mogae passes away at 86 after decade of stable leadership
item.description: Former Botswana President Festus Mogae has died at the age of 86, prompting the government to declare three days of national mourning. Mogae, who served as the country's third president from 1998 to 2...

item.title: Several hostages taken at savings bank in Sinzig, Germany; situation stable
item.description: German police reported a hostage situation at a savings bank branch in the western town of Sinzig, Rhineland-Palatinate, on Friday, May 8, 2026. The incident began at 0700 GMT in the town centre, wher...

item.title: Senator Abdul Ningi Resigns from PDP After Over 20 Years of Membership
item.description: Senator Abdul Ahmed Ningi, wey dey represent Bauchi Central Senatorial District, don formally resign im membership from di People’s Democratic Party (PDP). Di senator write resignation letter go PDP C...

item.title: Viral Guess the Party quiz reveals difficulty of identifying UK local election candidates by appearance
item.description: A new online game called Guess the Party has gone viral in the UK, challenging players to identify the political party of local election candidates solely from their photographs. Created by Sam Hamill...

item.title: Main findings from the first week of Australia's antisemitism royal commission
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, established following the December 2025 Bondi terror attack, has concluded its first week of hearings focusing on the experiences of Jewish Au...

item.title: Australian women charged with IS-related offences remain in custody after returning from Syria
item.description: Janai Safar, a 32-year-old Australian woman, has been denied bail in Sydney after being charged with joining Islamic State (IS) in Syria. Safar was arrested at Sydney airport following nearly a decade...

item.title: Tanzania positions itself to grow bee products exports to Asia with global market at 18 billion dollars
item.description: Tanzania is working hard to take advantage of the growing global bee products market, which is now valued at about 18 billion US dollars. The country wants to send more honey, beeswax and other bee it...

item.title: Labour suffers heavy losses in local elections as Starmer accepts responsibility
item.description: Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer has acknowledged responsibility for a series of disappointing local election results, which have seen the party lose hundreds of councillors and eight local authorities ...
